chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D-5000 of 2025 (Mst. Shama Shoaib versus Federation of Pakistan & others) Date Order with signature of Judge Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Abdul Mobeen Lakho 1. For order on office objection 2. For hearing of main case 19.1.2026 Mr. Shaukat Ali Shehroze advocate for the petitioner Ms. Mehreen Ibrahim, DAG Mr. Abdul Samad, Law Officer of NADRA --------------------- ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – Petitioner Mst. Shama Shoaib has filed this Constitutional Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking directions to respondents No.5 and 6 to notify respondent No.2 regarding the revocation and cancellation of her second CNIC No.42101-4514305-7 issued in the name of Shabana Sohail. She also seeks directions for the removal of her name from the blacklist, as the second CNIC has already been revoked/cancelled by respondents No.5 and 6 in accordance with their policy and legal formalities. Counsel for NADRA has submitted that the petitioner held dual CNICs 42201-1048747-6 and 42101-4514305-2 with different particulars. In 2012, she cancelled CNIC No.42201-1048747-6 and retained CNIC No. 42101-4514305-2 (tracking ID No.103181051341, Ayesha Manzil), with forms and affidavit attached. The petitioner now holds only CNIC No. 42101-4514305-2. Other issues fall within the jurisdiction of the civil Courts. The NADRA Law Officer confirmed that CNIC No.42101-4514305-2 shall be retained and forwarded to the competent authority for removal of the petitioner’s name from the blacklist.

Other issues fall within the jurisdiction of the civil Courts. The NADRA Law Officer confirmed that CNIC No.42101-4514305-2 shall be retained and forwarded to the competent authority for removal of the petitioner’s name from the blacklist. Learned DAG also sought disposal of this petition in view of the above statement. We have heard the learned counsel for the parties and perused the record with their assistance. The petitioner initially held dual CNICs, but she voluntarily cancelled one (42201-1048747-6) in 2012 and retained only CNIC No.42101-4514305-2. The second CNIC, which was purportedly issued in the name of Shabana Sohail, has already been revoked/cancelled by respondents No.5 and 6 following all legal 2 formalities. The remaining CNIC (42101-4514305-2) is valid and in the petitioner’s possession. Since the revocation of the second CNIC has been properly effected and all necessary legal steps complied with, there is no legal impediment to the petitioner’s name being removed from the blacklist, subject to all just exceptions as provided under the law. Further, issues beyond the issuance and cancellation of CNICs fall within the jurisdiction of the civil Courts if agitated by the aggrieved party. Accordingly, with the consent of the parties, and without touching its merits, the petition is disposed of with the direction that CNIC No.42101- 4514305-2 be retained, and the competent authority shall remove the petitioner’s name from the blacklist if there is no any impediment under the law. However, the aforementioned proposition ensures compliance with NADRA’s policy while upholding the petitioner’s lawful rights. JUDGE JUDGE Zahid/*
